rationale: The page contains detailed information about commercial Earth Observation (EO) satellite constellations and Earth's polar orbits, including technical specifications, mission details (Pixxel, Fram2), and applications. This is high-quality content for generating science and technology questions.
cleanedText:
4.7. COMMERCIAL EARTH OBSERVATION (EO) SATELLITE CONSTELLATION
Why in the News?
Pixxel successfully launched three new Firefly satellites aboard the SpaceX NAOS Falcon 9 mission completing the first phase of its commercial constellation.
More on the News
• PixxelSpace India-led Consortium has won the IN-SPACe proposal to build India’s first fully indigenous commercial Earth Observation Satellite System (EOSS) under PPP model.
o PixxelSpace India consortium comprises Piersight Space, Satsure Analytics India, and Dhruva Space.
o Public-Private Partnership (EO-PPP) model: The private sector will design, build, and operate the EOSS while, the government will provide strategic, technical, and policy support.
• Also, LEAP-1 mission of Dhruva Space’s, hosting two In-Orbit Demonstration (IOD) missions, was launched aboard the SpaceX NAOS Falcon 9 mission.
About the proposed Earth Observation Satellite System (EOSS)
• A constellation of 12 state-of-the-art EO satellites planned to be launched over the next 5 years.
o It will be equipped with panchromatic, multispectral, hyperspectral, and microwave SAR sensors.
• Deployment period: Four years
About EO Satellites
• Also called as Earth remote sensing satellites.
• They are designed to collect information about activities on Earth, both natural and artificial, including physical, chemical, biological and human systems.
Applications of Earth Observation Satellites
• Earth Sciences: Cartography & Oceanography, Meteorology & Space Science
• Environment & Wildlife: Biodiversity & Migration Patterns, River Erosion & Water Mapping
• Disaster Management: Monitoring and forecasting Floods, Earthquake & Cloud Bursts, Weather Forecasting
• Agriculture & Fisheries: Crop Estimation & Disease Detection, Agricultural Loss Assessment, Fish Shoal Monitoring
• Geology & Resources: Mineralogy Mapping, Geological Monitoring
• Spatial Planning: Infrastructure Planning, Transportation Networks
4.8. EARTH’S POLAR ORBIT
Why in the News?
Mission titled "Fram2" has been launched using Dragon spacecraft of SpaceX.
More on the News
• It will carry out a series of experiments, including the first X-ray in space and the cultivation of mushrooms in microgravity.
• It will be the first human spaceflight to cruise directly over Earth's Polar Regions.
About Earth’s polar orbit
• A polar orbit is when a satellite orbits Earth by passing over the North and South Poles.
o A deviation of 10 degrees over north and south poles is still classified as a polar orbit.
• Height: Polar orbits are a type of low Earth orbit, typically between 200 to 1000 km in altitude.
• Significance: A spacecraft orbiting over the poles can observe the entire planet as it spins underneath.
o The path is particularly useful for weather, mapping and spy satellites.
• Issue: Launching rocket into polar orbits requires greater fuel since rockets cannot harness Earth’s rotational speed.
